title: Level 613
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 613: "The Shattered Neighborhood"}} {{Class Variable}} Level 613 is the 614th level of the backrooms. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=A photograph of Level 613 taken by a wanderer.JPG|caption=fig 1.0 A photograph of Level 613 taken by a wanderer.}} Level 613 consists of what looks like fragments of town areas, usually suburban or middle-income neighborhoods scattered around in a desert-like terrain. All of these different neighborhoods are connected through a main road that stretches infinitely in both directions. Unlike most reported levels of the backrooms, Level 613 has various themes within the areas of the level. The differences between the areas range from suburban homes to city centers with high-rise apartments. All of said buildings suddenly stop and drop into the desert at a certain point, which makes it look like these town areas are actual fragments or torn-off pieces from complete city areas. The Desert of Level 613 doesn't appear to have anything to search or explore. It is a barren wasteland that imitates the environmental conditions of a desert from [[The Frontrooms]] and can also change its terrain and/or the color of the sand and rocks. The interiors of the level's buildings are mostly vacant and abandoned, with no furniture and nearly no ornamentation. It has been observed that wanderers are uncomfortable with the structure of the buildings and feel trapped together. Most of these buildings have rotting water and electrical installations, and various mold colonies can be seen in them and in entities that take advantage of this, so while entering the buildings is dangerous, they are effective in finding resources, as they can be found in bottled [[Object 1|Almond Water]] in some places. The level seems to fix things like broken items, stolen objects, or damage to the buildings. How this happens has yet to be discovered. All of the buildings are explorable, yet nothing of significance has been found in shops or buildings on the level. The type of architecture that will be seen appears to be random, and every fragment has differences from the others, no matter how uncannily similar they are to each other. The most important feature of the level to be taken into account during exploration is the day-night cycle in the level. Explorations of the level have shown that no matter how much time passes, the day-night cycle doesn't move, and instead, different areas experience different times of the day. All of these areas are often referred to as "time zones" due to the difference in the time of day.
===Time Zones=== |-| Daytime= Areas that live in the daytime are completely safe to explore, and they are devoid of any harmful entities and/or hazards. These areas are decently common and are perfectly habitable, usually containing a small playground with a fountain of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] that works occasionally. What makes these fountains work and not work is unknown, and it appears to happen at random. |-| Noon= Areas that live the noon are also devoid of harmful entities. However, long exposure to the sun can cause hyperthermia, even if it is unlikely to happen. The playground structure also appears in this area. |-| Afternoon= Areas that live in the afternoon contain a minimal amount of harmful entities. The playground structure doesn't appear in this area, but no environmental hazards are found. |-| Evening= The evening areas contain more entities compared to the afternoon areas. The playground structure doesn't appear, and no environmental hazards can be found. The evening areas contain significantly more entities in larger buildings, and [[Entity 4|Deathmoth]] nests can be found in rooms of the buildings that have sealed-off windows. |-| Night= Night areas contain a tremendous amount of entities, and the danger of hypothermia is present. The playground structure also appears here, but the fountains constantly pour out [[Object_12|Liquid Pain]] instead of Almond Water. Similar to evening areas, Night areas also have Deathmoth nests in the buildings, and the buildings seem to house even more entities.

{{Blurimage|file=An image of the connecting road.png|caption=fig 2.0 An image of the Connecting Road.}} The connecting road is the most notable landmark in the level, as it connects the fragments of neighborhood or town areas in Level 613. There are lamp posts with even distances between each other on both sides of the road. The light poles seem to change gradually as new types of architecture come closer, the same as the road and the sidewalk. Some wanderers have reported cars occasionally driving through this road, Yet it hasn't been confirmed, and the origin of these sightings is unknown. Even if it can change, the general distance between two fragments is around 5 kilometers.or 3 miles. Another remarkable thing about the level is that the darkness on the level greatly affects insanity and mental health more compared to other levels of the Backrooms. Where this effect is most noticeable are the Buildings and night areas on Level 613. As an example, shadow figures can be seen appearing and disappearing inside the buildings, sneaking from the corners of streets, and appearing more than a distance away from the person; although they resemble those of [[Level 188]], wanderers have been reported to feel paranoia at these moments and put a great burden on their hearts, for these reasons, cases of in-level heart attacks are high. ==Entities== Sightings of [[Entity List|Entities]] such as [[Entity 8|Hounds]] outside of the buildings have been confirmed alongside [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] in more dark, damp, and enclosed areas of Level 613. [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] can also be found lurking in the dark alleys or the buildings, even if it is a rare sighting. Specifically in night areas of Level 613, many wanderers claimed to see [[Entity 5|Clumps]] although it isn't yet confirmed. ==Colonies and Outposts== Despite a large amount of effort, No colonies or outposts were set up in Level 613 due to its ability to regenerate and fix everything that wasn't supposed to be there. All of the attempts to create bases, even in safe locations were impossible because the equipment in the base disappeared in what is thought to be around 2 days. ==Entrances and Exits== === Entrances === Not many locations have been reported to lead to Level 613. However, the most notable and consistent entrance is through finding a gate in [[Level 11]] in the shape of a door with the words "Fragmenta mundi..."Which means "Fragments of the World..." on it. Other discovered exits include: * [[No-Clipping]] through a torn up painting depicting [[The Frontrooms]] In [[Level 5]] can lead one to Level 613. * Running deep Into [[Level 9]], one might find a static surface at the side of a house. Noclipping into the static could lead to Level 613. * In [[Level 33]], if one enters a subway train and finds the windows show an endless sunset instead of tunnels, the next stop will drop one into an abandoned subway station in Level 613. * Taking the bus in [[Level 69]] can take one to the middle of The Connecting Road of Level 613. === Exits === The only known exit to this level is by finding a fractured mirror in one of the Night areas. Doing so will lead one to one of four levels: [[Level 0]], [[Level 5]], [[Level 37]], and [[Level 399]]. Whether or not there is any connection to these levels is unknown. But they have an even chance of happening. {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:AfterAllWhyNot|AfterAllWhyNot]]}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
